answer: 1. Cyclize 4-bromo-2-cyanobenzaldehyde with hydrazine hydrate under acidic conditions to form 6-bromo-3-cyanoindazole. 2. Convert 5-bromo-2-methylbenzoic acid to the acid chloride with SOCl2 and amidate with cyclopropylamine to afford 5-bromo-2-methyl-N-cyclopropylbenzamide. 3. Borylate the aryl bromide via Miyaura borylation (B2pin2, Pd(dppf)Cl2, KOAc) to generate the boronic ester. 4. Suzuki couple this boronate with 6-bromo-3-cyanoindazole (Pd(PPh3)4/K2CO3, dioxane/H2O) to yield the target molecule, then purify.

1. Perform a Suzuki coupling of 2,3,5-trichloropyridine with cyclopropylboronic acid using Pd(dppf)Cl2 and K3PO4 in dioxane/water at 80–90 °C to substitute the C-2 chlorine with a cyclopropyl group, yielding 2-cyclopropyl-3,5-dichloropyridine. 2. Subject this intermediate to SNAr by deprotonating 2,2-difluoroethanol with NaH (or K2CO3) in DMF, adding the pyridine to install the 2,2-difluoroethoxy group at C-3 and obtain 2-cyclopropyl-3-(2,2-difluoroethoxy)-5-chloropyridine. 3. Synthesize methyl 3-hydroxy-4-chloro-2-fluorobenzoate from the corresponding acid, then couple it to the remaining C-5 chloride via Buchwald–Hartwig O-arylation (Pd2(dba)3, XPhos, K3PO4, toluene, 100 °C) to form the diaryl ether methyl ester. 4. Hydrolyze the ester with LiOH or NaOH in THF/MeOH/H2O to obtain the target acid CC(F)(F)Oc1cc(Oc2cc(F)c(C(=O)O)cc2Cl)cnc1C1CC1.
